title: Google kills Tenor GIF API, forcing changes at X, Discord, and more
gist: Google shut down the free Tenor GIF API on June 30, 2026, forcing X, Discord, WhatsApp and Bluesky to migrate to alternatives like Klipy and Giphy. Klipy, co-founded by Tenor's original founder and backed by Google, is emerging as the main beneficiary.

insights.claim: Google discontinued the Tenor GIF API on June 30, 2026, ending third-party GIF access for X/Twitter, Discord, WhatsApp, and Bluesky, which had relied on it invisibly.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Free infrastructure APIs from big platforms are single points of failure; downstream products should diversify or self-host critical dependencies.
insights.quote: As part of an ongoing effort to focus resources on enhancing our core products, we’ve made the decision to sunset the Tenor API on June 30, 2026

insights.claim: Users blamed the platforms (X, Discord) rather than Google for lost GIFs because most never realized GIF access came from a third-party API, masking the true source of dependency risk.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Reputational fallout from upstream API shutdowns lands on the consumer-facing platform, not the vendor that pulled the plug.
insights.quote: Most users didn’t understand easy GIF access was provided by a third-party API and have directed their resulting anger at the platforms themselves.
insights.locator: To be clear, Tenor still exists

insights.claim: Google, with nearly 200,000 employees and over $130 billion in 2025 profit, likely killed Tenor's API because it was free and offered no path to monetization rather than for lack of resources.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Signals Google will keep pruning unmonetizable free services; builders on Google free tiers face ongoing deprecation risk.
insights.quote: The real problem was probably that Tenor was free, and Google didn’t see a way it could make money from a GIF API.
insights.locator: Google, a company with nearly 200,000 employees

insights.claim: Klipy, whose founders include original Tenor founder Frank Nawabi and which raised $3.8 million with Google as an investor, is winning GIF integration at Discord, WhatsApp, and Bluesky.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Google's shutdown effectively hands market share and traffic to a startup it funds; watch Klipy as the consolidating GIF-infrastructure play.
insights.quote: Frank Nawabi, who founded Tenor and sold it to Google, is one of the folks behind Klipy. The startup recently raised $3.8 million in funding, and one of the investors was, you guessed it, Google.
insights.locator: Users of these websites will see a different selection

insights.claim: X migrated off Tenor by June 20, 2026 (confirmed by Nikita Bier), while Discord tested both Giphy and Klipy before defaulting most users to Klipy.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Migration timelines show platforms had ~5 months of warning; GIF vendor selection is now an active competitive battleground.
insights.locator: Websites that relied on Tenor have been shifting
